title: lappula occidentalis var. cupulata
intro: Lappula occidentalis var. cupulata, also known as Western stickseed, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lappula occidentalis var. cupulata include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory congestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
